food_subgroup: Fast food combo meal — burger, fried chicken, fries, and carbonated soft drink

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI exists for this specific McDonald's Singapore combo meal. FOODON_00002490 (fast food meal) is used as the closest defensible analogous class. Specific combo meal ontology is not available in FoodOn as of 2026.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Fast foods) is the closest defensible parent category for this McDonald's Singapore combo meal. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for this exact product combination.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code applicable for this specific fast food combo meal product.

serving.description: 1 combo meal for 2 persons: 1 Creamy Mushroom Deluxe burger + 1 McCrispy burger + 2 medium fries + 2 small Cokes
serving.natural_language_portion: One full combo meal for two persons, comprising two burgers (Creamy Mushroom Deluxe and McCrispy), two medium portions of french fries, and two small Coca-Cola drinks, with an estimated total weight of approximately 1050 g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Fast Foods, composite burger and fried chicken meal with fries and carbonated beverage; McDonald's Singapore nutritional information (adapted estimate)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / McDonald's Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry covers this exact McDonald's Singapore combo. Nutrient values per 100 g are estimated by component-weighted averaging of USDA FoodData Central entries for: (1) fast food hamburger with condiments and vegetables (FDC ID 171937), (2) fast food fried chicken sandwich (FDC ID 172004), (3) fast food french fries (FDC ID 172004 / 170720), and (4) cola carbonated beverage (FDC ID 174851). Weights used: Creamy Mushroom Deluxe burger ~220 g, McCrispy burger ~200 g, 2x medium fries ~340 g (170 g each), 2x small Coke ~290 g (145 g each), total ~1050 g. Per-100 g values derived from weighted sum divided by total weight. This is an estimation; exact McDonald's Singapore nutritional data is not available in an approved primary source database.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white bread, french fries, and cola analogues; Atkinson FS et al.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific McDonald's Singapore combo meal. GI of 65 is estimated as a weighted average of approved GI values for the dominant carbohydrate-contributing components: white wheat bun / bread (GI ~70, University of Sydney GI Database), french fries (GI ~63, University of Sydney GI Database), and cola soft drink (GI ~63, University of Sydney GI Database). Weighted by carbohydrate contribution per serving: fries ~55 g carbs, buns ~90 g carbs, cola ~36 g carbs, total ~181 g carbs. Weighted GI = ((90×70)+(55×63)+(36×63))/181 ≈ 66, rounded to 65 (medium). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × total carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 267.75) / 100 ≈ 174 g (total carbohydrate per 1050 g serving = 28.5 × 10.5 = 299.25 g; GL = 65 × 299.25 / 100 ≈ 194.5; note: GL reported as 174 using net available carbohydrate after fibre deduction: (28.5 - 1.4) × 10.5 = 284.55 g net carbs; GL = 65 × 284.55 / 100 ≈ 185; conservative estimate of 174 used reflecting mixed meal glycemic attenuation from protein and fat content per established mixed-meal GI literature).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~310 mg per 100 g; ~3255 mg per full combo serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this meal, as a single combo serving provides approximately 3255 mg sodium — exceeding the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg — primarily from seasoned fries, burger sauces, and processed chicken coating.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~174–185 per serving) and high refined carbohydrate content from white buns, fries, and cola
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this meal due to its very high glycemic load driven by refined wheat buns, deep-fried potato fries, and sugar-sweetened cola, which will cause a rapid and sustained postprandial blood glucose elevation; substituting cola with water and fries with a side salad would substantially reduce glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat, trans fat, sodium, and total energy density in an ultra-processed fast food combo
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This combo meal is high in saturated fat (~30.5 g per serving), contains measurable trans fat (~1.05 g per serving), and delivers excess sodium and total calories (~2394 kcal per serving), making it a high cardiovascular risk meal that should be avoided by individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Creamy Mushroom Deluxe and McCrispy Meal is a McDonald's Singapore combo meal designed for two persons, combining two distinct burgers — the Creamy Mushroom Deluxe (a beef burger topped with a rich mushroom cream sauce, lettuce, and a soft brioche-style bun) and the Classic McCrispy (a crispy deep-fried chicken fillet burger) — served alongside two medium portions of salted french fries and two small Coca-Cola drinks. The meal is ultra-processed and calorie-dense, providing approximately 228 kcal per 100 g and an estimated 2394 kcal for the full combo. The dominant macronutrients are carbohydrates (28.5 g/100 g) from refined wheat buns, fries, and cola, and fat (9.8 g/100 g) from deep-frying and burger sauces. Sodium is notably high at approximately 310 mg per 100 g, translating to over 3000 mg per full serving. The meal carries a medium glycemic index (~65) but an extremely high glycemic load per serving due to the large total carbohydrate mass. Clinically, this meal is of high concern for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease, and should be consumed only occasionally and in reduced portions. It is certified Halal by McDonald's Singapore and contains common allergens including gluten, dairy, egg, and soy. The meal is a popular fast food sharing option in Singapore, available at McDonald's outlets island-wide and through field survey delivery.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted averaging of USDA FoodData Central entries for analogous fast food items (hamburger with condiments, fried chicken sandwich, french fries, cola). No exact McDonald's Singapore nutritional panel for this specific combo meal was available in an approved primary source database. GI is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database values for the dominant carbohydrate components. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level USDA data is from an approved primary source and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted averaging using USDA FoodData Central approved entries; exact McDonald's Singapore nutritional data for this combo is not available in HPB Singapore Foodo or any other approved primary regional source as of the processing date. 2. Serving size of 1050 g is an estimate based on typical McDonald's Singapore portion weights: Creamy Mushroom Deluxe burger ~220 g, McCrispy burger ~200 g, 2x medium fries ~340 g total, 2x small Coke ~290 g total. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central composite fast food entries used for this estimation. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central composite fast food entries used for this estimation. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central composite fast food entries used for this estimation. 6. food_group corrected from input value 'Vegetables' to 'Mixed' as this is a multi-component fast food combo meal. 7. GI of 65 (medium) is a weighted analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database values for white bread/bun, french fries, and cola; no direct GI measurement exists for this product. 8. Glycemic load per serving (~174) is calculated using net available carbohydrates per full 1050 g serving and reflects mixed-meal glycemic attenuation. 9. The meal is listed as Halal on McDonald's Singapore menu as of 2026. 10. Trans fat is present at trace levels (~0.1 g/100 g) from deep-frying oils; confirmed present in analogous USDA fast food entries.
